data.title: Ondo Launches Day-One Tokenized IPO Access Onchain
data.description:
Ondo Global Listing offers non-U. S. investors day-one tokenized access to NYSE and NASDAQ IPOs across Ethereum, Solana, and BNB Chain.
source: https://coincu.com/crypto/ondo-launches-day-one-tokenized-ipo/

data.title: Mediterranean Shipping Company: Will impose emergency fuel surcharge
data.description: Mediterranean Shipping Company (MSC) will impose an emergency fuel surcharge on all cargo shipped from the Mediterranean and Black Sea regions to the Indian subcontinent, the Red Sea, and East Africa. MSC: From March 16th until further notice, the emergency fuel surcharge for cargo on the Mediterranean and Black Sea to East Africa route will be adjusted to US$60 per TEU and US$90 per refrigerated container. (Jinshi)

data.title: Software Sector Faces Selloff Challenges Impacting OpenAI
data.description:
The software industry is experiencing a significant selloff, posing challenges for companies like OpenAI. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting concerns about the sector's performance and its implications for major players. The downturn in software stocks has raised questions about the sustainability of growth for firms heavily reliant on artificial intelligence and other advanced technologies.

OpenAI, known for its cutting-edge AI developments, is among the companies affected by the market's volatility. The selloff reflects broader uncertainties in the tech industry, as investors reassess the value and potential of software companies amid changing economic conditions.

Market analysts are closely monitoring the situation, noting that the selloff could lead to strategic shifts within affected companies. The focus is on how these firms will adapt to the evolving landscape and maintain their competitive edge in a challenging environment.

The software sector's performance is crucial for the overall tech industry, influencing investment decisions and future growth prospects. As companies navigate these turbulent times, the emphasis is on innovation and resilience to overcome the current challenges.

data.title: Crypto Founder Says Bitcoin, Ethereum, XRP Suffer Quantum Threat Risks, but XRP Has an Edge
data.description:
A prominent crypto founder says Bitcoin, Ethereum, and XRP all suffer quantum threat risks, but XRP may have an advantage over the others. Discussions about the impact of quantum computing on blockchain security have picked up the pace as researchers assess whether future quantum machines could break the cryptographic systems used by major networks. Visit Website
source: https://thecryptobasic.com/2026/03/07/crypto-founder-says-bitcoin-ethereum-xrp-suffer-quantum-threat-risks-but-xrp-has-an-edge/

data.title: The Israeli military claims it launched airstrikes on Iranian missile storage facilities with dozens of warplanes.
data.description: The Israel Defense Forces (IDF) issued a statement saying that the IDF launched a new round of airstrikes against Tehran and central Iran, deploying more than 80 warplanes to attack multiple military targets, including missile storage facilities. The statement said the airstrikes were "intended to bolster Iran's missile launch and defense capabilities." Targets included an underground facility used for storing ballistic missiles, containing military bunkers and a command center. Hundreds of military personnel were present at the time of the attack. The IDF also attacked a storage facility of Iranian missile forces containing military bunkers and launch devices, as well as missile launch sites in several regions of Iran. The IDF also attacked Imam Hussein University in Iran. The IDF stated that the university is the main military academy of the Iranian Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) and houses "various military facilities" of the IRGC. (CCTV News)

data.title: Iran's General Staff: Strikes against the US and Israel will continue.
data.description: A spokesperson for the General Staff of the Iranian Armed Forces stated that strikes against the United States and Israel will continue. The spokesperson said that Iran has so far struck all bases that have been the source of aggression against Iran; those countries that have not provided space and facilities to the United States and Israel are not Iran's targets and will not be targets in the future. (CCTV News)

data.title: Iran Continues Strikes Against U.S. and Israel
data.description: Iran's armed forces spokesperson announced that the country will persist in its strikes against the United States and Israel. According to Jin10, the spokesperson stated that Iran has targeted all bases identified as sources of aggression against the nation. Countries that do not provide space and facilities to the U.S. and Israel are not and will not be targets of Iran's actions.

data.title: Cardano's Selling Pressure Decreases by Nearly 50%
data.description: On-chain data indicates a significant reduction in Cardano's selling pressure, with the Spent Coins Age Band metric decreasing from approximately 171.42 million to 89.97 million coins. According to NS3.AI, this decline, coupled with a bullish divergence on the 12-hour RSI and a local profitability bottom, may facilitate a rebound attempt over the weekend. ADA is currently trading near a support level of $0.255–$0.258, while resistance zones are identified between $0.270 and $0.294 as potential targets for upward movement.

data.title: Kurds Considered for Role in Iran Regime Change Efforts
data.description: The Kurds are being evaluated as a potential ground force to facilitate regime change in Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, drawing parallels to the situation in Libya in 2011, which raises concerns about the potential outcomes of such an approach. The strategy involves leveraging the Kurds' position and capabilities to influence political shifts in Iran, reminiscent of past international interventions. The implications of this strategy are being closely analyzed, considering historical precedents and regional dynamics.

data.title: Iran Reports Extensive Damage to Civilian Infrastructure Amid Ongoing Conflict
data.description: Iran's Red Crescent Society has reported significant damage to civilian infrastructure across the country following attacks by the United States and Israel. According to Jin10, the organization's chairman stated that a total of 6,668 civilian facilities have been affected. This development highlights the ongoing tensions and the impact on Iran's civilian areas. The situation continues to evolve as geopolitical dynamics play out in the region.

data.title: Iran Temporarily Closes Stock Market Amid Economic Uncertainty
data.description: Iran's stock market has been temporarily closed until further notice, according to Iran's Tasnim News Agency. This decision was announced by Iran's Minister of Economy, reflecting ongoing economic challenges in the country. According to Jin10, the closure aims to address current market volatility and stabilize the financial environment. The move comes amid broader economic uncertainties affecting Iran, as the government seeks to implement measures to safeguard its financial systems. The duration of the closure has not been specified, leaving investors and market participants in anticipation of further updates from the authorities.

data.title: AI Investment Surge Diverts Funds from Crypto Startups
data.description:
Several venture capitalists have warned that the growing interest in artificial intelligence is redirecting investments away from crypto startups. According to Odaily, Charles Chong, Vice President of Strategy at BlockSpaceForce, noted that investors now have "real alternatives with faster revenue visibility," prompting crypto teams to work harder and founders to be more precise in areas such as defensiveness and profit models.

Data from DefiLlama indicates that crypto startups raised $128 million in the first week of March, with total funding reaching nearly $2.5 billion in 2026. This week's investors include Founders Fund, Ripple, Y Combinator, Wintermute, and Sequoia Capital, primarily focusing on payment, trading, and DeFi infrastructure sectors.

The top three funding events this week are: Latin American fintech company ARQ completed a $70 million Series B round led by Sequoia Capital and Founders Fund, with an annual transaction volume exceeding $10 billion and approximately 2 million users; Crossover Markets secured $31 million in a Series B round led by Tradeweb Markets, with participation from Ripple and Wintermute, valuing the company at around $200 million, and operating the institutional digital asset trading network CROSSx; hybrid derivatives exchange QFEX raised $9.5 million in a seed round led by Yuri Sagalov of General Catalyst, with participation from Y Combinator and Paul Graham.

data.title: VCs warn that the AI ​​boom is siphoning funds away from cryptocurrency startups.
data.description: Several venture capitalists have warned that the AI ​​craze is diverting funds away from crypto startups. Charles Chong, VP of Strategy at BlockSpaceForce, stated that investors now have "real alternatives with faster revenue visibility," and crypto teams need to work harder, with founders needing to be more precise in areas such as defensiveness and monetization models. DefiLlama data shows that crypto startups raised $128 million in the first week of March, bringing their total funding since 2026 to nearly $2.5 billion. Investors this week included Peter Thiel's Founders Fund, Ripple, Y Combinator, Wintertermute, and Sequoia Capital, primarily investing in payments, trading, and DeFi infrastructure. The top three funding events this week: Latin American fintech company ARQ completed a $70 million Series B funding round led by Sequoia Capital and Founders Fund. The company has an annualized trading volume of over $10 billion and approximately 2 million users. Crossover Markets completed a $31 million Series B funding round led by Tradeweb Markets, with participation from Ripple and Wintermute. The company operates CROSSx, a digital asset trading network for institutions. The hybrid derivatives exchange QFEX completed a $9.5 million seed funding round led by Yuri Sagalov of General Catalyst, with participation from Y Combinator and Paul Graham.

data.title: Canada's First Tokenized Bond Issued After Successful Blockchain Pilot
data.description:
Canada has successfully completed a pilot program testing distributed ledger technology in bond markets, resulting in the issuance of the country's first tokenized bond. According to Cointelegraph, the Bank of Canada announced the completion of this initiative, known as Project Samara, on Friday. The project involved collaboration between the Bank of Canada, Export Development Canada, Royal Bank of Canada, and TD Bank Group, aiming to determine if blockchain-style infrastructure could enhance the processes of bond issuance, trading, and settlement.

During the pilot, Export Development Canada issued a $100 million Canadian dollar bond with a maturity of less than three months to a select group of investors. This security was issued, traded, and settled on a distributed ledger platform, with payments processed using wholesale central bank deposits instead of commercial bank money. The platform, built on Hyperledger Fabric, allowed participants to manage the entire lifecycle of the security, including issuance, bidding, coupon payments, redemption, and secondary trading. It also integrated separate ledgers for cash and bonds to enable near-instant settlement.

The pilot revealed both benefits and challenges in adopting distributed ledger systems for capital markets. Participants noted improvements in operational efficiency and data integrity but also faced governance, regulatory, and integration challenges. Researchers concluded that while distributed ledger systems could enhance settlement efficiency and reduce counterparty risk, broader adoption might be hindered by infrastructure and regulatory obstacles.

Canada's pilot program is part of a broader trend among governments and financial institutions exploring blockchain-based systems for traditional financial assets. In 2018, the World Bank issued a two-year A$110 million "Bond-i" debt instrument, considered the first bond recorded on a blockchain. In 2022, the Monetary Authority of Singapore launched Project Guardian to study distributed ledger technology in wholesale financial markets, with early pilots exploring decentralized finance applications for lending and borrowing tokenized bonds. In 2023, Hong Kong issued a tokenized green bond using distributed ledger infrastructure, facilitated by the Hong Kong Monetary Authority, with further digital bond offerings planned for 2024 and 2025. Additionally, the World Bank issued a Swiss franc digital bond on the SIX Digital Exchange in 2024, settled using wholesale central bank digital currency provided by the Swiss National Bank.

data.title: India Permits Iranian Warship to Dock Amid Tensions
data.description: India has granted permission for an Iranian warship to dock at one of its ports. Bloomberg posted on X, this development comes shortly before a separate Iranian frigate was reportedly sunk by a U.S. submarine in the Persian Gulf. The docking of the Iranian vessel in India highlights the complex geopolitical dynamics in the region, as tensions continue to simmer between Iran and the United States. The incident underscores the strategic importance of naval operations and alliances in the Persian Gulf area.

data.title: Bitcoin's Bear Market Cycle May Be in Mid-Stage, Analyst Suggests
data.description: CryptoQuant analyst Axel recently shared insights on the X platform, indicating that the NUPL-MVRV harmonic composite indicator has reached 0.33. According to PANews, historical data suggests that the bottom of market cycles typically occurs around the 0.5 range. The chart reveals an upward trend in the bear market cycle, suggesting that Bitcoin might be in the mid-stage of the current bear market cycle. This implies that extreme selling conditions are becoming more moderate. However, the indicator remains significantly above the historical bottom range, indicating that a full market sell-off has not yet been confirmed.

data.title: Nigeria's Central Bank Confirms Compliance with New Capital Requirements
data.description: Nigeria's central bank announced that 30 lenders have successfully met the new capital requirements threshold set by the institution. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the central bank's statement regarding the compliance of these financial institutions. The announcement comes ahead of the March deadline, ensuring that the banks are well-prepared to adhere to the updated regulatory standards. This development is part of the central bank's efforts to strengthen the financial sector and enhance its stability. The compliance of these lenders indicates a positive step towards achieving the central bank's objectives in maintaining a robust banking system.
